$comment: @generated by generate_api_artifacts — do not edit. Regenerate: buck run fbcode//monarch/hyperactor_mesh:generate_api_artifacts @fbcode//mode/dev-nosan -- fbcode/monarch/hyperactor_mesh/src/testdata

ActiveHandler.description: One handler with in-flight invocations, at the HTTP boundary.

name.description: Handler name (e.g. a Python endpoint method name).

oldest_since.description: Start time of the oldest in-flight invocation (ISO 8601 string).

AdminInfo.description:
Self-identification payload returned by `GET /v1/admin`.

Construct via [`AdminInfo::new`]. AI-1, AI-2, AI-3 are live
invariants. The relationship between `host` and `url` is a
constructor guarantee — `AdminInfo::new()` rejects URLs with no
host, so `host` always derives from `url` at construction.

host.description: Hostname the admin HTTP server bound on (derived from `url`).

AdminInfo.title: AdminInfo

ApiError.description:
Structured error response following the gateway RFC envelope
pattern.

details.description:
Additional context about the error. Schema is permissive
(any valid JSON) — `details` is a domain-specific escape
hatch. Do not assume a fixed shape.

ApiErrorEnvelope.description: Wrapper for the structured error envelope.
ApiErrorEnvelope.title: ApiErrorEnvelope

Execution.description:
An actor's in-flight handler execution at the HTTP boundary.

`null` (absent) means the actor does not report execution
(unsupported), not idle (EX-1). `complete == false` means the
per-handler detail was momentarily unavailable on that read while
`active_count` stays authoritative (EX-2).

active_handlers.description:
Per-handler detail, oldest-first; a prefix of the N oldest when
`truncated` (EX-4).

complete.description: `true` iff the per-handler detail was captured on this read.

truncated.description: `true` iff `active_handlers` is a prefix of the N oldest.

FailureInfo.description: Structured failure information for failed actors.

is_propagated.description: Whether this failure was propagated from a child.

occurred_at.description: When the failure occurred (ISO 8601 timestamp string).

root_cause_actor.description: Actor that caused the failure (root cause).

root_cause_name.description: Display name of the root-cause actor, if available.

InboundOrdering.description:
Mesh-admin presentation of inbound ordering state.

Rollups marked `returned_*` are computed over `sessions` only and
are LOWER BOUNDS when `snapshot_complete == false` (IO-6).
`known_session_count` is the only rollup that totals returned +
skipped sessions.

enabled.description: `true` when reorder buffering is enabled for this sender.

known_session_count.description:
IO-5: `sessions.len() + skipped_session_count`. The only rollup
that totals returned + skipped sessions.

returned_buffered_message_count.description:
IO-6: sum of `buffered_count` OVER RETURNED sessions.
Reorder-buffer scope only (independent of `queue_depth`; see
IO-3). Lower bound if `!snapshot_complete`.

returned_buffered_session_count.description:
IO-6: sessions with `buffered_count > 0` AMONG RETURNED
sessions. Lower bound if `!snapshot_complete`.

returned_max_buffered_count.description:
IO-6: max of `buffered_count` OVER RETURNED sessions. Lower
bound if `!snapshot_complete`.

skipped_session_count.description: Sessions held by a concurrent send at snapshot time.

NodePayload.description:
Uniform response for any node in the mesh topology.

Every addressable entity (root, host, proc, actor) is represented
as a `NodePayload`. The client navigates the mesh by fetching a
node and following its `children` references.

`identity`, `children`, and `parent` are plain reference strings.
`as_of` is an ISO 8601 timestamp string.

as_of.description: When this payload was captured (ISO 8601 timestamp string).

children.description:
Child node reference strings the client can URL-encode and
fetch via `GET /v1/{reference}`.

identity.description: Canonical node reference identifying this node.

parent.description: Parent node reference for upward navigation.

NodePayload.title: NodePayload

NodeProperties.description:
Node-specific metadata. Externally-tagged enum — the JSON
key is the variant name (Root, Host, Proc, Actor, Error).

oneOf.description: Synthetic mesh root node (not a real actor/proc).
A host in the mesh, represented by its `HostAgent`.
Properties describing a proc running on a host.
Runtime metadata for a single actor instance.

execution.description:
In-flight handler execution. `null` means the actor does not
report execution (unsupported), not idle. See EX-* in
`hyperactor_mesh::introspect`.

instance_id.description:
Stable per-instance Uuid::now_v7() identity assigned at
`Instance::new` (string form).

queue_depth.description:
Accepted handler work not yet dequeued by the actor loop
(PD-5a/b in `hyperactor::proc`). Independent diagnostic from
`inbound_ordering`; no arithmetic contract -- see IO-3 in
`hyperactor::introspect`.

oneOf.description: Error sentinel returned when a child reference cannot be resolved.

OrderingSessionSnapshot.description: Per-session reorder-buffer snapshot at the HTTP boundary.

buffered_count.description: Messages held in the reorder buffer waiting for a seq gap.

last_released_seq.description:
Highest seq released from the reorder buffer into the actor
work queue.

sender.description:
Session owner actor address (string form; `None` only in rare
bypass paths).

actor_work_queue_depth_high_water_mark.description: Maximum proc-wide queue depth since startup (PD-6, eventually consistent).

actor_work_queue_depth_max.description: Max per-actor queue depth (live actors only).

actor_work_queue_depth_total.description: Sum of per-actor queue depths (live actors only).

last_nonzero_queue_depth_age_ms.description: Milliseconds since proc-wide queue depth was last observed non-zero (PD-7, wall clock).

ProcessMemoryStats.description:
Memory stats of the hosting OS process (DTO mirror of
`ProcessMemoryStats`).

process_rss_bytes.description: RSS of the hosting OS process (bytes).

process_vm_size_bytes.description: Virtual memory size of the hosting OS process (bytes).

PySpyFrame.description: A single frame in a py-spy stack trace.

filename.description: Absolute path to the source file.

is_entry.description: Whether this frame is an entry point (e.g., module `__main__`).

locals.description: Local variables captured in this frame, if available.

PySpyLocalVariable.description: A local variable captured in a py-spy frame.

addr.description: Memory address of the Python object.

arg.description: Whether this variable is a function argument.

PySpyProfileOpts.description:
Public JSON-facing options for a py-spy profile capture.

Deserialized from the HTTP POST body. Validated and converted to
`ValidatedProfileRequest` before any actor messaging.

See PP-1 in `introspect` module doc.

duration_s.description:
Sampling duration in whole seconds. py-spy `--duration`
accepts integers only. Must be >= 1; upper bound enforced
at runtime by `MESH_ADMIN_PYSPY_MAX_PROFILE_DURATION`.

rate_hz.description: Sampling rate in Hz. Must be 1..=1000.

PySpyProfileOpts.title: PySpyProfileOpts

PySpyResult.description:
Result of a py-spy stack dump request.

See PS-2, PS-4 in `introspect` module doc.

oneOf.description: Successful stack dump with structured traces.

binary.description: Path or name of the py-spy binary that produced the dump.

pid.description: OS process ID that was dumped.

warnings.description:
Non-fatal warnings from the capture (e.g., flag
fallbacks). Empty when the capture completed without
caveats.

oneOf.description: py-spy binary not found in environment.

searched.description: Candidate paths that were tried before giving up.

binary.description: Path or name of the py-spy binary that failed.

exit_code.description: Exit code from the py-spy process, if available.

pid.description: OS process ID that was targeted.

PySpyResult.title: PySpyResult

PySpyStackTrace.description: A single thread's stack trace from py-spy `--json` output.

active.description: Whether the thread is actively running (not idle/waiting).

owns_gil.description: Whether the thread currently holds the GIL.

pid.description: OS process ID that owns this thread.

dump_id.description: Unique identifier for the stored dump.

info.description: Address-walking introspection API for a Monarch actor mesh. See the Admin Gateway Pattern RFC.
info.title: Monarch Mesh Admin API

get.description: Returns the admin actor's identity, proc placement, hostname, and URL. Used for placement verification and operational discovery.
Returns the effective CONFIG-marked configuration entries from the target process. Routes to ProcAgent (worker procs) or HostAgent (service proc).

200.description: ConfigDumpResult — sorted list of config entries

404.description: Proc not found or handler not reachable

get.description: Runs py-spy against the target process and returns structured stack traces. Routes to ProcAgent (worker procs) or HostAgent (service proc).

200.description: PySpyResult — one of Ok, BinaryNotFound, or Failed

404.description: Proc not found or handler not reachable

get.summary: Py-spy stack dump for a proc

post.description: Runs py-spy against the target process, stores the result in the dashboard's DataFusion pyspy tables, and returns the dump_id.
post.summary: Trigger py-spy dump and store in telemetry

post.description: Runs py-spy record against the target process for the requested duration and returns an SVG flamegraph. Timeout scales with duration_s.

400.description: Bad request (invalid duration/rate or malformed proc reference)

404.description: Proc not found or handler not reachable

500.description: Internal error (profile failed or SVG generation failed)

503.description: Service unavailable (py-spy not available on target host)

post.summary: Profile a proc and return SVG flamegraph

post.description: Forwards a SQL query to the Monarch dashboard's DataFusion engine. Requires telemetry_url to be configured.

400.description: Bad request (invalid SQL or missing sql field)

post.summary: Proxy SQL query to the telemetry dashboard

503.description: Service unavailable (at capacity, retry with backoff)
Service unavailable (at capacity, retry with backoff)

get.summary: Resolve a reference to a NodePayload
